personal.name: Md Asif Jawed
personal.description: Building scalable backend systems, distributed architectures, and production-grade SaaS products.
about: I fell in love with programming and I have at least learnt something, I think… 🤷‍♂️
I am fluent in classics like Node.js, TypeScript, and Postgres.
My field of interest's are building new Web Technologies and Products and also in areas related to Deep Learning.
Whenever possible, I also apply my passion for developing products with Node.js and Modern Javascript Library and Frameworks like React.js and Next.js.

experience.description: Architected and scaled core backend payment infrastructures and real-time gaming services.
bullets: Spearheaded the migration of legacy monolithic architecture to highly scalable, event-driven microservices using Node.js and AWS.
Engineered robust payment gateway integrations and core wallet systems, successfully processing millions of high-concurrency transactions securely.
Optimized real-time gaming services, reducing websocket latency by 40% to ensure seamless player experiences during peak traffic.

experience.description: Developed and maintained highly performant APIs for diverse SaaS and Web3 applications.
bullets: Designed and implemented high-performance RESTful APIs for multiple enterprise SaaS platforms, decreasing average response times by 30%.
Optimized complex PostgreSQL database queries and implemented caching layers using Redis, drastically reducing database load.
Integrated Web3 capabilities and smart contract interactions for blockchain-centric client projects, ensuring robust security standards.

projects.title: TarbiyahOS
projects.description: A comprehensive SaaS platform designed to streamline educational operations, engineered for high velocity, scalability, and optimal performance.
bullets: Architected a strict multi-tenant data isolation strategy using PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) via Supabase.
Leveraged Next.js App Router and React Server Components to reduce client-side JS bundles by 60%, achieving near-instant Time To Interactive.
Implemented real-time data sync using PostgreSQL triggers and designed a seamless Single Sign-On (SSO) authentication workflow.

works.title: Building TarbiyahOS: A Comprehensive SaaS Architecture Case Study
works.intro: Building TarbiyahOS was a unique engineering challenge that focused heavily on creating a multi-tenant SaaS application designed to perform reliably under heavy load. The decision to use Next.js for the frontend and Supabase for the backend stemmed from the need for high velocity without sacrificing stability.

sections.heading: Why I Built It
sections.content: TarbiyahOS was built to solve the operational chaos experienced by modern educational institutions. Existing platforms were either too rigid, overly bloated, or extremely slow. The goal was to build a streamlined, highly performant, and developer-friendly alternative that respects tenant data isolation.

sections.heading: Architecture Decisions
sections.content: When evaluating the tech stack for TarbiyahOS, I needed to optimize for three core pillars: developer experience, performance, and built-in security for multi-tenancy. I bypassed traditional ORMs and custom-built REST APIs in favor of a modern, server-centric approach.

sections.heading: Why Next.js
sections.content: Choosing the Next.js App Router allowed me to heavily utilize React Server Components. This drastically reduced the client-side JavaScript bundle, resulting in a faster Time To Interactive (TTI) for heavy dashboard views and incredible SEO for public-facing landing pages.

sections.heading: Why Supabase
sections.content: Supabase was chosen for its direct Postgres connection and built-in features. It provided out-of-the-box authentication (frictionless SSO and magic-link logins) and allowed for real-time updates to connected clients using PostgreSQL triggers without maintaining complex websocket servers.

sections.heading: Why PostgreSQL & RLS Design
sections.content: PostgreSQL was the natural choice for relational integrity. Instead of managing tenant isolation in application code, I architected a strict multi-tenant data isolation strategy using PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) via Supabase. This enforces tenant boundaries directly at the database level, meaning data leaks between tenants are mathematically impossible even if application code fails.

sections.heading: Analytics & Monitoring Stack
sections.content: Visibility into user behavior and system health is critical for a SaaS product. I integrated PostHog heavily for feature flags and comprehensive product analytics. For observability, Sentry and LogRocket were critical for capturing edge-case errors and replaying user sessions in a distributed environment.

sections.heading: SEO Strategy
sections.content: The SEO strategy relied heavily on Next.js Server-Side Rendering (SSR). By strictly separating the public marketing pages (statically generated) from the private dashboard (dynamic server components), I ensured lightning-fast load times for search engine crawlers, while JSON-LD schema provided structured data.

sections.heading: Lessons Learned
sections.content: The primary challenge was managing complex state across the application without relying on heavy global state managers like Redux. By relying on React Server Components for data fetching and React Query for client-side caching, the architecture mental model remained simple and predictable. Looking back, optimizing database indexes early and thoroughly testing RLS policies saved us from potential performance bottlenecks as the platform scaled.
